item.title: The Skyline Problem
item.author: Author
item.description: UVa 105 - The Skyline Problem This problem tests the application of priority queue which is implemented with min-heap. Min-heap takes O(logn) to insert a new node. It takes O(1) to extract current minimum value and O(logn) to sort again. With this data structure, I can iterate the boundaries of building in ascending order. My idea is recording all boundaries and associated heights in priority queue and current existing height in multiset.
